steps.title: Factor from x terms
steps.content:
Factor $5$ from the two terms involving $x$.
$$
y=5x^2-bx-8
$$
$$
y=5(x^2-\frac{b}{5}x)-8
$$

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Half of $\frac{b}{5}$ is $\frac{b}{10}$, and its square is $\frac{b^2}{100}$.
$$
y=5(x^2-\frac{b}{5}x+\frac{b^2}{100}-\frac{b^2}{100})-8
$$

steps.title: Move the extra constant
steps.content: Rewrite the perfect square and distribute the subtracted term. $$ y=5(x-\frac{b}{10})^2-\frac{5b^2}{100}-8 $$ $$ y=5(x-\frac{b}{10})^2-8-\frac{b^2}{20} $$ The answer is A.
